adjuncts_ranked_candidates.action_name: Low-dose aspirin chemoprevention (GI cancer, ATM het + paternal-line GI cancer)

trigger.summary: DDR-impaired carrier with paternal GI cancer history; CAPP2 RCT in DDR-deficient (Lynch) population shows durable CRC reduction with daily aspirin.

adjuncts_ranked_candidates.analog_population: Lynch syndrome (CAPP2 RCT) — closest hereditary DDR-impaired analog for ATM het; supplemented by Rothwell pooled data in general population.
adjuncts_ranked_candidates.mechanism: ATM haploinsufficiency impairs DSB repair, raising epithelial cancer risk (CRC ~1.5-2x). Aspirin reduces CRC via COX-2/PGE2 suppression and platelet-mediated tumor-cell cloaking, with the largest absolute benefit in DDR-impaired epithelium where baseline risk is elevated.
adjuncts_ranked_candidates.intervention: Aspirin 81 mg PO daily after shared decision-making; baseline H. pylori test and bleeding-risk review first; reassess at 5 yr (CAPP2 effect emerges ~5 yr in).
adjuncts_ranked_candidates.tracking_metric: Adherence; annual CBC; GI bleeding symptoms; colonoscopy findings.
adjuncts_ranked_candidates.urgency: Not urgent — onset of benefit is 5+ yr, so starting in the late 40s aligns benefit with the rising slope of GI-cancer risk.

spareq.s_reason: GI cancer (CRC, pancreatic) is the load-bearing domain in this profile.
spareq.p_reason: ATM het + paternal GI cancer = composite elevation; aspirin operates on the pathway of concern.
spareq.a_reason: Outcome-redirect: CAPP2 showed ~50% CRC reduction in Lynch at 10-yr; even attenuated effect in ATM is meaningful absolute risk shift.
spareq.e_reason: GI bleeding ~0.5-1%/yr baseline; H. pylori-negative, normotensive, no NSAID baseline → favorable. ASPREE noted no net benefit in low-risk elderly, but this patient is not the ASPREE population — he carries an elevated-risk allele.
spareq.q_reason: CAPP2 RCT in analog DDR-impaired population (Lynch, n=861, 10-yr f/u) + Rothwell pooled-trial data; no direct ATM RCT exists.

citations.supports: CAPP2 10-yr/20-yr registry: 600mg aspirin daily reduced CRC ~50% in Lynch syndrome — DDR-impaired analog.
Systematic review of aspirin chemoprevention/treatment evidence across cancers.
Recent review of low-dose aspirin CRC chemoprevention evidence.

adjuncts_ranked_candidates.action_name: Alcohol minimization (≤2 standard drinks/wk or abstain)

trigger.summary: Acetaldehyde produces structural DSB-class genomic alterations; ATM haploinsufficiency reduces capacity to resolve them, producing super-additive risk in DDR-deficient epithelium (esp. pancreas, upper GI).

adjuncts_ranked_candidates.mechanism: Ethanol → acetaldehyde → DNA-protein crosslinks and DSBs that depend on ATM-dependent HR for repair. ATM-haploinsufficient cells tolerate these lesions less well, magnifying per-drink absolute risk in pancreatic and GI epithelium.
adjuncts_ranked_candidates.intervention: Cap alcohol at ≤2 standard drinks/wk; preferentially zero. No 'social-drinking' rationalization given allele.
adjuncts_ranked_candidates.tracking_metric: Self-reported weekly drinks; PEth if rigor desired.
adjuncts_ranked_candidates.urgency: Indefinite habit-level lever; benefit begins at exposure reduction.

spareq.s_reason: Pancreatic and upper-GI cancer severity is high; absolute prevention value matters.
spareq.p_reason: ATM het amplifies acetaldehyde-DSB pathway specifically.
spareq.a_reason: Trajectory-anchor: dose-response cohort shows threshold effect; mechanism strong, allele-specific RCT absent.
spareq.q_reason: Mechanism (acetaldehyde structural DSBs, 2026) + cohort dose-response data; no allele-specific RCT.

citations.supports: Long-term acetaldehyde exposure elevates structural genomic alterations (DSB-class) — mechanism.
Threshold dose-response between alcohol and young-onset pancreatic cancer in nationwide cohort.
DDR pathway (incl. ATM) centrality in pancreatic carcinogenesis.

trigger.summary: Family history of GI cancer (gastric in differential) + planned chronic aspirin makes one-time H. pylori test cost-effective and modifies gastric cancer trajectory.

adjuncts_ranked_candidates.analog_population: First-degree relatives of gastric cancer patients (Choi et al. RCT analog).
adjuncts_ranked_candidates.mechanism: H. pylori is a Class I gastric carcinogen acting via chronic inflammation, atrophy, and intestinal metaplasia. Eradication in family-history-positive individuals reduces incident gastric cancer; in ATM-het epithelium, eliminating a chronic DSB-inducing inflammatory exposure is leverage-positive. Also de-risks aspirin GI bleeding.
adjuncts_ranked_candidates.intervention: One-time stool antigen or urea breath test; if positive → triple/quadruple therapy per local resistance; confirm eradication at 4-8 wk.

spareq.s_reason: Gastric cancer is high-severity; intestinal metaplasia is silent until late.
spareq.p_reason: Family hx GI cancer + ATM het + planned aspirin = three independent reasons.
spareq.a_reason: Outcome-redirect: Choi 2020 NEJM RCT showed eradication in first-degree relatives reduced gastric cancer incidence ~55%.
spareq.e_reason: Antibiotics if positive; no active harm signal.
spareq.q_reason: RCT in family-history population + multiple meta-analyses.

citations.supports: Meta-analysis: family-history gastric cancer subjects benefit most from H. pylori screening/eradication.
Narrative review of H. pylori eradication importance for gastric cancer prevention.
APAGE 2026 task-force recommendations on H. pylori-associated premalignant surveillance.

trigger.summary: ATM-haploinsufficient cells show measurably impaired DSB repair after ionizing radiation; cohort data link low-dose occupational radiation to GI cancer mortality.

adjuncts_ranked_candidates.analog_population: US radiologic technologists cohort (low-dose ionizing radiation).
adjuncts_ranked_candidates.mechanism: ATM is the apex DSB-sensor kinase; heterozygous loss reduces repair fidelity. IR-induced DSBs in ATM-het epithelium have higher residual misrepair rates → carcinogenic dose-per-exposure is higher than population baseline.
adjuncts_ranked_candidates.intervention: Prefer MRI/US over CT where clinically equivalent; refuse low-yield CT; for required imaging request lowest-dose protocol; avoid 'just-in-case' whole-body CT screening packages.

spareq.a_reason: Trajectory-anchor: avoids preventable lifetime DSB load; per-exposure absolute effect small but cumulative.
spareq.e_reason: No harm — substituting to MRI/US where appropriate.
spareq.q_reason: Strong mechanism + occupational cohort linking low-dose IR to GI cancer mortality; no ATM-specific RCT.

citations.supports: Low-dose occupational ionising radiation associated with GI cancer mortality in US radiologic technologists 1983-2021.
ATM-pathway DDR deficiency relevance to pancreatic cancer.

adjuncts_ranked_candidates.action_name: Resistant starch (~30 g/day) for upper-GI/CRC chemoprevention

trigger.summary: CAPP2 RCT planned 10-yr follow-up showed resistant starch reduced non-CRC Lynch cancers — relevant signal for upper-GI in DDR-impaired carrier.

adjuncts_ranked_candidates.mechanism: Colonic fermentation of resistant starch → butyrate → HDAC inhibition, anti-inflammatory and pro-apoptotic effects in DDR-stressed epithelium; possible systemic effect on upper-GI cancers seen in CAPP2.
adjuncts_ranked_candidates.intervention: ~30 g/day resistant starch (cooked-then-cooled potato/rice, green banana flour, raw oats) sustained ≥2 yr; combine with aspirin (A1), do not substitute.

spareq.s_reason: Upper GI cancers are high-severity; CAPP2 saw signal there.
spareq.p_reason: DDR-deficient carrier with GI family hx — analog match imperfect.
spareq.a_reason: Trajectory-anchor: HR for non-CRC Lynch cancers ~0.54 in planned analysis.
spareq.q_reason: Single RCT in analog Lynch population (CAPP2 resistant starch arm, planned 10-yr follow-up).

citations.supports: CAPP2 planned 10-yr follow-up: resistant starch reduced non-CRC Lynch cancers.

contraindicated_in_optimized_patients.reason: SELECT trial showed increased prostate cancer incidence with α-tocopherol supplementation in primary prevention; ATM het already carries ~2x prostate cancer risk — actively harmful direction.

citations.supports: Context for ATM cancer-domain stacking; SELECT primary citation outside this run.

contraindicated_in_optimized_patients.reason: ATBC and CARET trials showed increased lung cancer in primary prevention. No place in an optimized profile.
Net IR exposure in an ATM-haploinsufficient patient is direction-of-harm; see A4. False-positive workup adds further IR.

research_gaps.description: Sulforaphane / broccoli-sprout extract for NRF2-mediated chemoprevention in ATM-het carriers — mechanistically plausible (NRF2 upregulation buffers oxidative DSB load), Qidong biomarker RCT exists in aflatoxin context, but no human outcome data in ATM het or DDR-deficient carriers.
research_gaps.what_would_close_it: Biomarker RCT in ATM-het cohort measuring urinary 8-oxo-dG and γH2AX foci over 6-12 mo on standardized sulforaphane dose.

research_gaps.description: Metformin chemoprevention in non-diabetic ATM het with pancreatic cancer family history.
research_gaps.what_would_close_it: RCT in CAPS-eligible high-risk pancreatic surveillance cohort with imaging endpoints.

research_gaps.description: Direct aspirin chemoprevention RCT in ATM heterozygotes (the present recommendation extrapolates from Lynch).
research_gaps.what_would_close_it: CAPP-style RCT enrolling ATM/CHEK2/PALB2 carriers with CRC and pancreatic endpoints.

considered_and_excluded.action_name: Annual MRI/MRCP + EUS pancreatic surveillance from age 50

considered_and_excluded.reason: below_evidence_floor for an Adjunct here — CAC=0, Lp(a) optimal, PREVENT 10-yr ASCVD ~1-1.5%; statin appropriately deferred per SoC bucket logic.

considered_and_excluded.action_name: Vitamin D supplementation for cancer prevention
considered_and_excluded.reason: below_evidence_floor — VITAL did not show pancreatic/CRC incidence benefit in primary prevention; not specific to ATM.
below_evidence_floor — MesaCAPP is an ongoing protocol, no outcome data yet.
covered_by_soc_bucket / standard ATM-het guideline space (NCCN earlier-PSA discussion).
